title: Global Market: Investors face triple test this week from geopolitics, rate outlook and tech earnings
description: Global markets brace for a crucial week. Geopolitical tensions involving Iran and the Strait of Hormuz are a major focus. Shifting interest rate expectations from the US Federal Reserve and European central banks add to uncertainty. Big Tech earnings, especially those related to artificial intelligence, will also shape investor sentiment. This convergence of factors promises significant market movement.
content: Global financial markets are heading into a pivotal week shaped by three dominant forces: escalating geopolitical tensions involving Iran, shifting expectations for interest rates, and continued momentum in artificial intelligence-driven growth, acco... [5688 chars]

title: College students wary of the job market are changing course in search of 'AI-proof' majors
description: The rise of artificial intelligence is prompting college students to second-guess their career paths. Students say that picking a major that’s AI-proof feels like shooting at a moving target, as they try to prepare for a job market that is changing rapidly. A surge of recent polls show that college students see AI as a threat to their job prospects, particularly those majoring in technology-related fields. Until recently, the narrative that guided many students was that learning to code would lead to good jobs. But students who followed that path and are now majoring in computer science, analytics and other popular STEM majors fear that AI is taking entry-level jobs.
content:
Two years ago, Josephine Timperman arrived at college with a plan. She declared a major in business analytics, figuring she'd learn niche skills that would stand out on a resume and help land a good job after college.
But the rise of artificial intel... [266 chars]

title: Bluechip safety no more? Infosys knocked out of India's top 10 valuable companies list after losing Rs 2 lakh crore
description: Infosys, once a market leader, has lost significant value and exited India's top 10 companies. Investors are concerned about future growth and client spending. Artificial intelligence presents both opportunities and challenges. The IT sector is undergoing a transformation, impacting established players. Infosys is adapting to these evolving market dynamics.
content: Infosys, once seen as a steady compounder in India's equity markets, is facing a sharp reassessment from investors. The company has lost over Rs 2 lakh crore in market value this year and slipped out of the list of India's 10 most valued companies, r... [6358 chars]
